content: LLM tracing security: LangSmith had CVE AgentSmith (CVSS 8.8) allowing a malicious agent to route all communications (including OpenAI API keys, prompts, attachments) through an attacker proxy. Traces log full inputs/outputs by default — if system prompt or document content contains what looks like credentials, they appear in traces. Mitigation: LangSmith supports input/output masking via env vars and regex patterns, or use Microsoft Presidio for PII detection. This project already has redaction.py for logging, but external trace backends bypass it. Prefer local trace storage (Supabase) over external services like LangSmith for this free-tier project. OpenTelemetry overhead: ~1-5ms per span (negligible for LLM calls that take seconds), but SDK cold-start initialization can add 100-500ms on first serverless invocation.
